rag_rules: Repair validator diagnostics by adding only valid AGREE clauses to the implementation annex of Q.Impl
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope or AADL features
Do not add clauses that reference subcomponent ports or paths unless they are valid in the implementation scope
Do not add clauses that compare opaque data or Boolean values with numeric ranges or 0/1 equality
Do not add clauses that reference package-qualified identifiers or data types absent from the analyzed model
generic_rules: Repair validator diagnostics by adding only valid AGREE clauses to the appropriate annex scope
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope or AADL features
Do not add clauses that reference subcomponent ports or paths unless they are valid in the current scope
Do not add clauses that compare opaque data or Boolean values with numeric ranges or 0/1 equality
Do not add clauses that reference package-qualified identifiers or data types absent from the analyzed model
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation annexes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Use a type-level guarantee over visible ports, or an implementation-level assign when the left-hand side is a valid output
anti_patterns: Do not add clauses that reference unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations
Do not add clauses that point to implementation-level references or subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers
Do not add clauses that compare opaque data or Boolean values with numeric ranges or 0/1 equality
